title: Missing DDL_LOCK_TIMEOUT — DDL fails with ORA-00054 if lock unavailable
migration_snippet:
-- V2__add_column.sql
ALTER TABLE orders ADD (tracking_id VARCHAR2(50));
expected_feedback: Critical: DDL executed without DDL_LOCK_TIMEOUT. Without this setting, Oracle does not wait for locks — if any session holds even a brief lock on the table, the ALTER fails immediately with ORA-00054 (resource busy, acquire with NOWAIT specified). This is especially dangerous during peak hours when transient row locks are frequent. Fix: add ALTER SESSION SET DDL_LOCK_TIMEOUT = 3; before the DDL, which instructs Oracle to retry acquiring the exclusive DDL lock for up to 3 seconds instead of failing instantly.
